metadata.author: Habakkuk
metadata.subject: The Holy Judgment of God First on Israel by the Chaldeans and then on the Chaldeans by the Nations.

1: The burden which Habakkuk the prophet saw.
How long, O Jehovah, must I cry out, and You do not hear? I cry out to You, Violence! and You do not save.
Why do You make me see iniquity and look upon trouble? For destruction and violence are before me; and there is strife, and contention rises up.
2: I will stand at my guard post and station myself on the watchtower; and I will keep watch to see what He will say to me and what I will answer concerning my complaint.
And Jehovah answered me and said, Write the vision and engrave it clearly upon tablets, that he who reads it may run.
Behold, his soul is puffed up; it is not upright within him; but the righteous one shall live by his faith.
For the earth will be filled with the knowledge of the glory of Jehovah, as the waters cover the sea.
But Jehovah is in His holy temple; let all the earth keep silence before Him.
3: O Jehovah, I have heard the report of You and I am afraid. O Jehovah, revive Your work in the midst of the years; in the midst of the years make it known; in wrath remember mercy.
Though the fig tree does not blossom, and there is no fruit on the vines... Yet I will exult in Jehovah; I will rejoice in the God of my salvation.
Jehovah the Lord is my strength; and He makes my feet like hinds' feet and causes me to walk on my high places.

4.text: This crucial verse is the foundation for the New Testament revelation of justification by faith (Rom. 1:17; Gal. 3:11; Heb. 10:38). It indicates that the righteous man lives not by his circumstances, but by his trust in God.

2.text: The prophet's prayer is for God to manifest His power and life again among His people during the long 'years' of their captivity and suffering.

19.text: Hinds' feet are agile and sure-footed. To walk on high places signifies a life that transcends the trials and valleys of the earth through the strength of the Lord.
